fields.title: Grand Canyon Indigenous Displacement References Removed
fields.summary: National Park Service removed signs and exhibits referencing the forced displacement of Native Americans from Grand Canyon region when the National Park was created.

fields.title: Executive Order 14253: 'Restoring Truth and Sanity to American History'
fields.summary: President Trump signed Executive Order 14253 directing the Department of the Interior and National Park Service to remove exhibits about slavery, Indigenous genocide, civil rights, and climate science from national parks and federal lands.

fields.title: Muir Woods 'History Under Construction' Exhibit Removal
fields.summary: Trump administration removed 'History Under Construction' exhibit acknowledging Coast Miwok Indigenous history and the racist roots of conservation movements at Muir Woods National Monument.

fields.title: Stonewall National Monument LGBTQ+ History Altered
fields.summary: Trump administration removed and altered references to transgender and queer people at Stonewall National Monument — the birthplace of the modern LGBTQ+ rights movement.
fields.location: Stonewall National Monument, New York City

fields.title: President's House Slavery Exhibit Removal - Philadelphia
fields.summary: National Park Service workers used crowbars to remove the only federal historic site exhibit commemorating slavery in America — 'The President's House: Freedom and Slavery in the Making of a New Nation.'
fields.location: Independence National Historical Park, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania

fields.title: Glacier National Park Climate Change Science Removed
fields.summary: Officials removed descriptions and educational materials explaining how climate change is contributing to glacial loss at Glacier National Park.

fields.title: Little Bighorn Battlefield Native American Signage Removed
fields.summary: National Park Service removed or altered Native American–related signage at Little Bighorn Battlefield National Monument; tribal leaders and the Northern Cheyenne Tribal Council opposed changes to markers at the site. Reported by Military Times and others.
fields.location: Little Bighorn Battlefield National Monument, Montana

fields.title: Acadia National Park Climate and Indigenous Educational Signs Removed
fields.summary: Acadia National Park removed educational signs addressing climate change and Indigenous history, part of broader NPS interpretive changes under federal direction. Covered by Vermont Public and regional outlets.

fields.title: NMAAHC: Major Artifacts Rotated Off Display
fields.summary: NBC News and other outlets reported dozens of objects tied to slavery and civil rights were no longer on public view at the National Museum of African American History and Culture, including items linked to Harriet Tubman and Frederick Douglass, following an executive order on Smithsonian content; the Smithsonian cited standard rotations and loan practices.
fields.location: National Museum of African American History and Culture, Washington D.C.

fields.title: National Museum of American History: Impeachment Exhibit Revised
fields.summary: Reuters reported the National Museum of American History removed Donald Trump's name from an exhibit on presidential impeachment, restoring an earlier version of the display.
fields.location: National Museum of American History, Washington D.C.

fields.title: Federal Review of NPS Signs on Native Mistreatment and Climate
fields.summary: The Washington Post and Reuters reported national parks were directed to review and remove signage on mistreatment of Native Americans and on climate change, affecting many sites across several states.
fields.location: Department of the Interior / National Park System (national scope)

fields.title: Zion National Park: Indigenous and Climate Interpretation Targeted
fields.summary: Zion National Park in Utah was among sites named in coverage of removals or reviews of interpretive material on Indigenous displacement and climate, alongside other Southwest parks.

fields.title: Big Bend National Park Interpretive Content Reviewed
fields.summary: Big Bend National Park, Texas, was listed among parks where Native-related and climate-related interpretive content faced removal or review under the same federal initiative reported by Arizona Central, Reuters, and others.

fields.title: Philadelphia Lawsuit Over President's House Exhibit Removal
fields.summary: Philadelphia officials filed suit over the dismantling of slavery-related exhibits at the President's House site, citing a 2006 city–federal agreement; AP News and local outlets covered the dispute and judicial review.

fields.title: Tribes Protest Grand Canyon Native History Exhibit Changes
fields.summary: Arizona Republic reported tribal nations protested National Park Service actions targeting Native history exhibits and interpretation at Grand Canyon National Park.

fields.title: Redlining and Racial Covenants: HOLC 'Security Maps' (New Deal Era)
fields.summary: The federal Home Owners' Loan Corporation produced 'Residential Security' maps grading neighborhoods; 'D' areas were often redlined, excluding Black borrowers and shaping decades of segregated wealth. Documented by the Mapping Inequality project and historians.
fields.location: United States (federal program; maps for 200+ cities)

fields.title: Chinese Exclusion Act
fields.summary: Federal law barred most Chinese immigration and denied naturalization to Chinese residents—the first major U.S. law restricting immigration by nationality. Repealed in stages; remains a landmark of race-based immigration policy.

fields.title: Tulsa Race Massacre
fields.summary: A white mob destroyed Greenwood, Tulsa's prosperous Black district—killing hundreds and leveling homes and businesses. Long minimized in public memory; now widely taught as one of the worst episodes of racial terror in U.S. history.
